title: Why FIGS Stock Down 16.8 Percent After Raising Revenue Guidance
text: Despite raising 2026 revenue guidance after strong Q1, FIGS stock fell 16.8%. Market reacted negatively due to slower growth expectations despite Q1's impressive 28% YoY revenue growth. Raised full-year guidance of only 14-16% signaled significant deceleration, disappointing investors expecting sustained momentum. Underlying concerns included growing tariff exposure and slower customer growth guidance suggesting structural challenges despite near-term strength. High market expectations limited upside with investors anticipating even better guidance. Biggest risk identified as revenue growth slowing when promotions pulled back and demand proves less resilient. Stock decline reflects classic market dynamic where good news fails matching inflated expectations, with underlying business risks including tariff pressures and potential demand softness outweighing quarterly beat.
